channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Qatar Says Ras Laffan Blast Will Not Affect LNG Exports
item.description: Qatar said it doesn't expect liquefied natural gas exports to be impacted by an explosion at the Ras Laffan industrial complex which left 13 dead and 66 injured.

item.title: Pikka Oil Project in Alaska Reaches Continuous Production
item.description: The first production wells are now delivering 20,000 barrels per day gross.

item.title: Venezuela post-Maduro: will oil majors invest again?
item.description: Energy Technology’s new episode discusses Venezuela’s oil picture and the likelihood that foreign investment will spark a revival.

item.title: India set to witness substantial ethylene capacity additions by 2030
item.description: India is set to emerge as a frontrunner in global ethylene capacity additions by 2030, fueled by government initiatives and private and public CapEx.

item.title: BP, Eni Greenlight Cross-Block Project offshore Angola
item.description: The Greater PAJ Project will deploy a new FPSO with a nameplate capacity of 95,000 barrels per day of oil and a gas export capacity of 70 million cubic feet a day of gas, to be supplied to Angola LNG.

item.title: Chevron Wins 20-Year Contract to Power Microsoft Data Center
item.description: A project by Chevron and Engine No. 1 secured an agreement to supply natural gas-based electricity to a Microsoft data center project in West Texas for 20 years.

item.title: Santos brings first Pikka phase one wells online on Alaska’s North Slope
item.description: Santos has begun continuous production operations at the Pikka phase one oil project located on Alaska’s North Slope, with the initial production wells now online.

item.title: Traders Question How Much Iranian Oil Can Really Return to Market
item.description: US-Iran talks and a 60-day sanctions waiver have eased supply fears, but traders remain cautious on the outlook for Iranian oil exports. From War Shock to Weather Shock: Super El Niño Threatens Fresh Commodity Chaos -…

item.title: Analysts Project Oil, Gas Market Evolution to 2050
item.description: Analysts at BMI projected the evolution of oil and gas markets through to 2050 in a report sent to Rigzone recently.

item.title: Perenco, Partners Ink Gas Sales Agreement for Vietnam Project
item.description: 'Su Tu Trang Phase 2B will supply more than 600 billion cubic feet of gas over its life, supporting growing energy demand in Southeast Vietnam and contributing to the country's energy security'.

item.title: Uzbekistan Woos More Western Gas Investors
item.description: Uzbekistan is stepping up efforts to bring major European and U.S. energy companies into its natural gas sector, after last month securing investment from BP.

item.title: Novak: Russia Considers Complete Ban on Diesel Exports
item.description: Russia is considering a full ban on diesel exports as authorities scramble to stabilize the domestic fuel market amid refinery disruptions, rising prices, and supply shortages linked to Ukrainian attacks on energy…

item.title: China’s Antimony Ban Sent Prices Up 2,600%. Rare Earths Are Next
item.description: In 2024, China announced export controls on antimony. It’s a metal most people have never heard of, but antimony goes into more than 200 types of military munitions. Within weeks, the price went from $1,400 per ton…

item.title: Murphy Oil discovers oil at Bubale-1X well offshore Côte d’Ivoire
item.description: US-based energy company Murphy Oil has discovered oil at the Bubale-1X exploration well, located around 40 miles from the coast of Côte d’Ivoire in Block CI-709.

item.title: Azule and partners sanction $5.1bn Greater PAJ offshore Angola
item.description: Azule Energy and its partners, Sonangol E&P and Equinor, have taken the final investment decision (FID) for the Greater PAJ Project, a $5.1bn offshore oil development in Angola’s Blocks 31 and 31/21.

item.title: Tankers Emerge from Dark Mode amid Tentative Hormuz ReopeningTankers Emerge from
item.description: A growing number of oil tankers have been broadcasting their position and intention to pass through the Strait of Hormuz in recent hours, in a sign that a tentative recovery of traffic through the chokepoint is…

item.title: U.S. and Iran Signal Progress Toward Peace Deal
item.description: Iran and the United States cited progress in talks toward reaching a final peace deal, though comments from both sides highlighted the fragile state of negotiations. Hours after US Vice President JD Vance left…

item.title: Iran Quickly Pursues Top Oil Importers after US Waiver
item.description: Iran is racing to court some of Asia's largest oil buyers as a 60-day U.S. sanctions waiver takes effect.

item.title: Europe’s Battery Storage Installations Set to Quadruple by 2030
item.description: further accelerate through 2030 as utility-scale projects lead growth, the SolarPower Europe association said in a new report on Tuesday. Last year, the European battery storage market saw 36 gigawatt-hours (GWh) of new…

item.title: IOC's Tanker Tender Comes Up Empty as Hormuz Risk Lingers
item.description: Indian Oil Corporation (IOC), the biggest refiner in the country, did not receive any bids in a tender to charter three tankers to pick up crude and gas from the Persian Gulf and ship the volumes through the Strait of…
